texto: You'd have thought that after suffering such a loss nothing else would matter to her but that didn't seem to be how it worked. She was fearful about everything now. It was as if she had finally seen the awful power of fate, it's deviousness, the way it could wipe out in an instant the one thing you had been certain you could rely on, and now she was constantly looking over her shoulder, trying to work out where the next blow might fall.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Mary Lawson: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de enfrentar incertezas com ação. O trecho “You'd have thought that after suffering such a loss nothing else would matter to her but that did…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que trabalho, fear, loss pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de luto e trabalho.
